records.ety: [L. confiscatus, p. p. of confiscare to confiscate, prop., to lay up in a chest; con- + fiscus basket, purse, treasury. See Fiscal.]

defs: Seized and appropriated by the government to the public use; forfeited.
quotes: Lest that your goods too soon be confiscate.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Confiscated; p. pr. & vb. n. Confiscating.]

defs: To seize as forfeited to the public treasury; to appropriate to the public use.
quotes: It was judged that he should be banished and his whole estate confiscated and seized.
